methodsAttempted.query: site:unina.it Intelligenza Artificiale Federico II linee guida
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the Italian and English official AI guidelines pages and the attached PDF.
Search results were mainly lab, thesis, event, and publication pages; no separate university-wide AI privacy policy was found beyond the official guidelines PDF.
Verified www.unina.it as the official University of Naples Federico II domain from source branding and institutional footer text.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a university-wide AI guidelines announcement page and the attached official PDF, PG/2026/0005657, approved by the Academic Senate after a favorable Board of Directors opinion. Claims were limited to that university-wide guidance. Course listings, publication records, lab research pages, and event programme material were documented but rejected as insufficient policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale

artifacts.claimText: University of Naples Federico II has published university-wide first guidelines for use of artificial intelligence systems inside the university, approved by the Academic Senate after a favorable Board of Directors opinion.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim because the PDF explicitly states senate approval and university-wide internal scope.

artifacts.claimText: The guidelines list OpenAI ChatGPT Edu/Enterprise, Microsoft Copilot, and other CSI-approved tools under CRUI framework agreements as AI systems available to the university.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: L'Ateneo dispone dei seguenti sistemi di intelligenza artificiale autorizzati e gestiti secondo gli accordi CRUI in vigore: OpenAI - ChatGPT Edu / ChatGPT Enterprise; Microsoft Copilot; Eventuali altri strumenti di IA generativa o assistiva approvati dal CSI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guidelines list ChatGPT Edu/Enterprise, Microsoft Copilot, and other CSI-approved AI tools under CRUI agreements.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 5, Sistemi di intelligenza artificiale in dotazione all'Ateneo

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a tool-treatment claim. Wording avoids saying every listed tool is available for every use; the source ties use to institutional purposes and the guidelines.

artifacts.claimText: The guidelines state that AI systems used within the university must include contractual guarantees that user-provided data are not used to train LLMs except with specific authorization, and they prohibit entering special categories of personal data in prompts.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: I sistemi di intelligenza artificiale utilizzabili all'interno dell'Ateneo devono prevedere, nel relativo contratto o accordo di fornitura, la garanzia esplicita che i dati forniti dagli utenti non vengano utilizzati per l'addestramento dei modelli linguistici (LLM), salvo specifiche e puntuali autorizzazioni da parte del CSI e del DPO di Ateneo.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Contracts or supply agreements must guarantee that user-provided data are not used to train LLMs unless specifically authorized by CSI and the university DPO.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 5, Condizioni di utilizzo e tutela dei dati
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a privacy claim directly supported by section 5. The claim does not generalize beyond AI-system use covered by the guidelines.

artifacts.claimText: For teaching staff and similar roles, the guidelines allow AI as support for teaching preparation and content or exercise generation with human supervision and labeling, but prohibit using AI to perform evaluations.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Si può utilizzare l'IA a supporto della didattica, per la preparazione e l'erogazione di contenuti didattici e per la generazione di esercizi, domande, questionari, con l'obbligo di supervisione umana dell'output e delle fonti, nonché di etichettatura del materiale generato. È vietato l'impiego dell'IA per effettuare valutazioni.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI may support teaching materials and exercises with human supervision and labeling; use of AI to perform evaluations is prohibited.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 6.1, Per i docenti, anche a contratto, ricercatori e figure assimilabili
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because section 6.1 directly states the permitted teaching-support uses and the prohibition on AI for evaluations.

artifacts.claimText: For students and similar figures, the guidelines say AI may be used only as support for study and learning, and AI-generated content for assessments or theses is not permitted without explicit declaration and authorization from the responsible teacher.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Per gli studenti, l'IA può essere esclusivamente uno strumento di supporto allo studio e all'apprendimento... non è consentito ricorrere alla IA per generare qualsiasi contenuto destinato a prove di valutazione o tesi senza dichiarazione esplicita e senza l'autorizzazione del docente responsabile.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students may use AI only as support for study and learning; AI-generated assessment or thesis content requires explicit declaration and teacher authorization.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 6.2, Per gli studenti universitari e figure assimilabili
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as academic-integrity guidance. The claim keeps the scope to students and similar figures and preserves the source's declaration and authorization conditions.

artifacts.claimText: For research, the guidelines say AI can support research activities, but researchers remain fully responsible for originality, content, methodological quality, accuracy, ethics, and integrity, and an AI system cannot be considered an author or co-author of scientific publications.

citation.citationTitle: PG/2026/0005657 - Prime linee guida per l'utilizzo dei Sistemi di Intelligenza Artificiale

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: resta piena responsabilità dei ricercatori garantire che l'utilizzo della IA sia coerente con i principi di integrità scientifica della ricerca... Un sistema di intelligenza artificiale non può essere considerato autore o coautore di pubblicazioni scientifiche.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Researchers remain responsible for integrity and quality; an AI system cannot be considered an author or co-author of scientific publications.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a research claim because section 7 directly supports both researcher responsibility and the no-AI-authorship rule.

artifacts.claimText: The guidelines state that each user is responsible for correct AI-tool use in their area of competence and that failure to comply with the guidelines is a violation of behavioral obligations subject to disciplinary responsibility under applicable role-specific rules.

artifacts.title: University of Naples Federico II AI Guidelines Crawl Draft
artifacts.summary: Official University of Naples Federico II sources show a university-wide first AI guidelines document approved in late 2025 and circulated as PG/2026/0005657 in January 2026. The strongest claims concern guideline approval and scope, CRUI/CSI-authorized systems, privacy and prompt-data safeguards, teaching-assessment limits, student disclosure and authorization expectations, research responsibility including no AI authorship, and user responsibility for compliance.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The crawl found one university-wide AI guidelines source set. Rejected course, lab, publication, and event sources were not used as evidence.
Direct curl access to www.unina.it returned HTTP 403 during this run; Firecrawl successfully retrieved the public page and PDF without using login-gated or CAPTCHA-protected access.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
